skill.name: information-security-manager-iso27001
skill.description: Guides ISO 27001 and ISO 27002 information security management for healthcare, MedTech, and regulated technology teams.
skill.summary: Plan ISO 27001 ISMS work, security risk assessments, control implementation, and compliance readiness.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: AI review dismissed the static high-risk matches as false positives from ISO 27001 terminology, Markdown examples, and resource filenames. No executable shell commands, weak cryptographic implementations, network reconnaissance instructions, data exfiltration, or prompt injection attempts were found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Dismissed
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged lines contain ISO 27001, healthcare, asset, and documentation terminology. They do not implement MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any other weak cryptographic algorithm. Confidence: 0.97. Confidence reasoning: The exact lines are prose or placeholder documentation, and no cryptographic code or configuration is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is descriptive documentation only. No code, commands, or cryptographic parameters appear at these locations.

low_findings.title: External Command Static Matches Dismissed
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged backtick locations are Markdown code fences and inline filenames in a resources section. They do not invoke Ruby, a shell, or any command execution path. Confidence: 0.98. Confidence reasoning: The content is rendered documentation with static text only, and there is no executable command syntax or user input flow.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every listed location is Markdown formatting or a resource filename. No backtick shell execution pattern is present in executable code.

low_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance Static Match Dismissed
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged line discusses general security awareness training. It does not instruct scanning, probing, enumeration, or target discovery. Confidence: 0.95. Confidence reasoning: The context is an ISMS training program list, not operational reconnaissance guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a training topic with no network commands, tools, targets, or scanning steps.

content.value_statement: ISO 27001 implementation requires structured planning, risk assessment, and control evidence. This skill helps teams organize ISMS work for healthcare and regulated technology environments.
actual_capabilities: Structures ISO 27001 ISMS planning and implementation activities.
Guides information security risk assessment and treatment planning.
Organizes ISO 27002 control categories for governance discussions.
Covers healthcare, MedTech, cloud, and privacy-security considerations.
Supports incident management, security metrics, audits, and management review.
Frames certification readiness tasks and surveillance audit preparation.
limitations: Referenced detailed guides are placeholders or not included in this package.
It does not perform automated scans, evidence collection, or control testing.
It does not replace legal advice, a certification body, or a qualified auditor.
Regulatory requirements must be verified against current official sources.

use_cases.title: Plan an ISMS implementation
use_cases.description: Create a practical sequence for scope definition, policy work, risk assessment, controls, monitoring, and improvement.

use_cases.title: Prepare certification readiness
use_cases.description: Map certification preparation tasks, evidence needs, audit coordination, surveillance planning, and management review inputs.

use_cases.title: Assess healthcare security controls
use_cases.description: Review healthcare data, medical device, cloud, privacy, and incident response considerations against ISO 27001 expectations.

prompt_templates.title: Start an ISMS plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a starter ISO 27001 ISMS implementation plan for a healthcare software company with 80 employees.

prompt_templates.title: Build a risk assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft an information security risk assessment approach for patient data, cloud services, and internal business systems.

prompt_templates.title: Map control priorities
prompt_templates.prompt: Prioritize ISO 27002 control areas for a MedTech company preparing for certification within nine months.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare management review
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an ISO 27001 management review agenda with metrics, risk updates, compliance status, incidents, and improvement actions.

output_examples.input: Request: Create an ISO 27001 implementation roadmap for a HealthTech startup.
output: A phased roadmap covering scope, policies, asset inventory, risk assessment, control selection, operation, audit, and improvement.
Milestones identify owners, evidence needs, review points, and certification readiness checkpoints.

output_examples.input: Request: Review medical device cybersecurity controls for certification readiness.
output: A control review outline for device authentication, encryption, network segmentation, update management, monitoring, and incident response.
Gaps are grouped by risk impact, expected evidence, and suggested remediation activity.

output_examples.input: Request: Build a management review pack for the ISMS steering group.
output: A structured agenda with performance metrics, incident trends, risk treatment status, compliance updates, and improvement decisions.
The output includes action tracking guidance for accountable owners and follow-up dates.

best_practices: Start with a clear ISMS scope, asset inventory, and risk methodology before selecting controls.
Connect every control to risk treatment, evidence requirements, ownership, and review frequency.
Validate healthcare, privacy, and device requirements with current official guidance.
anti_patterns: Do not treat ISO 27001 as a checklist without risk-based control justification.
Do not use generic policies that ignore clinical data, devices, suppliers, and cloud responsibilities.
Do not claim certification readiness without evidence, internal audit results, and management review records.

faq.question: What standard does this skill focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on ISO 27001 ISMS implementation and ISO 27002 control guidance.

faq.question: Is this useful for healthcare companies?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes healthcare, MedTech, cloud, privacy, and incident management considerations.

faq.question: Can it certify an organization?
faq.answer: No. Certification must be performed by an accredited certification body.

faq.question: Does it include working automation scripts?
faq.answer: No. The included script is a placeholder and does not implement real automation.

faq.question: Can it replace a security auditor?
faq.answer: No. It supports preparation and planning, but human review and audit judgment remain required.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
